commands.author: xydt-tanshanshan

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): route memory embedding through generic resolution when provider has custom baseUrl
target.body:
## Summary

Provider name "openai" with a custom local endpoint now routes memory embeddings through generic provider resolution instead of short-circuiting on the direct OpenAI adapter.
- Problem: `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` returns the direct "openai" adapter unconditionally when the provider name matches, ignoring `api` or `baseUrl` hints in the provider config. Users with `providers.openai.baseUrl = "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"` get embeddings routed to `api.openai.com`.
- Solution: When a direct adapter match has provider config with `baseUrl` or `api` fields, consult `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId` for a better-matching adapter. The resolved adapter ID (`actualProvider`) is threaded through `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` → `createEmbeddingProvider` so the runtime adapter lookup matches the resolved config.
- What changed: `src/agents/memory-search.ts` (+31 lines, `actualProvider` field + generic resolution), `src/agents/memory-search.test.ts` (+87 lines, 3 config-resolution cases), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts` (+4 lines, `actualProvider` in adapter lookup), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` (+45 lines, 2 create-path runtime cases), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-provider-state.ts` (+2 lines, pass-through), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts` (+3 lines, thread `actualProvider` from settings into `createEmbeddingProvider`)
- What did NOT change: Config schema, adapter registrations, auth/resolution logic, existing behavior for providers without `baseUrl`/`api`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93396
- Related to #93505, #93533 (complementary baseURL alias fixes)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a user configures `models.providers.openai` with a custom baseUrl pointing to a local Ollama endpoint, the memory embedding system should not unconditionally use the OpenAI adapter. The existing generic resolution path (`resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId`) already handles provider routing via `api` field, but `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` bypasses it because the direct "openai" adapter match returns immediately. Furthermore, even when the config resolver picks the right adapter, the runtime `createEmbeddingProvider` path receives the original provider ID (`"openai"`) and its own adapter lookup finds the direct OpenAI adapter first.

This fix addresses both layers:
1. Config resolution: consult generic resolution before committing to a direct adapter match
2. Runtime creation: thread the resolved adapter ID (`actualProvider`) through `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig` → `createEmbeddingProvider` so the adapter lookup uses the resolved identity

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Provider name "openai" with `api: "ollama"` must route `createEmbeddingProvider` to the Ollama adapter at runtime, not just in config resolution
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw dev worktree, Node.js, Ubuntu 24.04, `fix/memory-embedding-provider-routing-93396` branch
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx proof-93853.mts` exercises the full config→runtime chain by registering adapters, resolving config through `resolveMemorySearchConfig`, and calling `createEmbeddingProvider` with the resolved `actualProvider`. Unit tests: `npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts -t "actualProvider"`, `npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts`, `npx vitest run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts`.

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `proof-93853.mts` (below) demonstrates the full config→runtime chain. A standalone script registers `openai` and `ollama` adapters, configures `providers.openai.api: "ollama"`, resolves config via `resolveMemorySearchConfig` (which sets `actualProvider: "ollama"`), and passes it into `createEmbeddingProvider` — the same entry point called by `MemoryIndexManager.loadProviderResult`:

```console
$ node --import tsx proof-93853.mts

── Register adapters: openai + ollama ──
  registered openai  (model: text-embedding-3-small)
  registered ollama  (model: nomic-embed-text)

── Config: openai provider → api:ollama ──
  providers.openai.api  = "ollama"

── resolveMemorySearchConfig ──
  resolved.provider   = "openai"
  resolved.model      = "nomic-embed-text"
  resolved.actualProvider = "ollama"

── createEmbeddingProvider (the real runtime entry point) ──
  input:  provider="openai", actualProvider="ollama"
  output: provider.id="ollama", requestedProvider="openai"
  PASS createEmbeddingProvider returned ollama adapter (id: ollama)

── Verdict ──
  PASS config resolution sets actualProvider = "ollama"
  PASS createEmbeddingProvider(provider:"openai", actualProvider:"ollama") → ollama adapter
  PASS the runtime path now matches the config resolution path

── ALL CHECKS PASSED ──

$ npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts -t "actualProvider"
 ✓ actualProvider routes createEmbeddingProvider to the resolved adapter  8ms
 ✓ absent actualProvider preserves direct provider lookup  2ms

$ npx vitest run extensions/.../embeddings.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)   Tests  10 passed (10)

$ npx vitest run src/agents/memory-search.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)   Tests  38 passed (38)
```

- Observed result after fix: `resolveMemorySearchConfig` sets `actualProvider: "ollama"` when `providers.openai.api === "ollama"` and an Ollama adapter is registered. `createEmbeddingProvider({ provider: "openai", actualProvider: "ollama" })` returns the Ollama adapter with `provider.id: "ollama"`, not the OpenAI adapter. The full config→runtime chain is now connected: `ResolvedMemorySearchConfig.actualProvider` → `loadProviderResult` → `createEmbeddingProvider` → `getAdapter(actualProvider)` → Ollama adapter. When `actualProvider` is absent or no matching adapter exists, the original direct provider lookup is used (backward compatible).

- What was not tested: Live Ollama endpoint E2E (the fix operates at the adapter-resolution layer). The `baseURL` (uppercase) field name alias is handled by sibling PR #93505, not this PR.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

`getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` in `src/agents/memory-search.ts:194` returns the direct adapter unconditionally without checking provider config routing hints (`baseUrl`, `api`). The existing generic resolution path in `embedding-provider-config.ts` already handles these hints, but is only reached when no direct adapter exists.

## Regression Test Plan

40 tests pass (35 existing + 3 config-resolution + 2 create-path runtime). The 5 new tests verify:

Config-resolution layer (`memory-search.test.ts`):
1. `actualProvider` is set: openai provider with `api: "ollama"` resolves `actualProvider: "ollama"`, and the model comes from the Ollama adapter
2. `actualProvider` unset when no adapter registered: openai with only `baseUrl` routes to `"openai-compatible"` but since no adapter exists for that id, `actualProvider` stays undefined → direct adapter is used
3. `actualProvider` unset for plain config: openai with no hints has no `actualProvider` — backward compatible

Create-path runtime layer (`embeddings.test.ts`):
4. `actualProvider` routes `createEmbeddingProvider` to the correct adapter: openai provider with `actualProvider: "ollama"` → ollama adapter is invoked, not openai
5. `actualProvider` absent preserves direct provider routing: no regression for existing callers

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for users who use the canonical "openai" provider with default api.openai.com endpoint. Users who configure `providers.openai` with `api: "ollama"` will now get embeddings from the Ollama adapter.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Node: v22+
- Branch: `fix/memory-embedding-provider-routing-93396`

### Steps

1. Configure `models.providers.openai` with `api: "ollama"` and `baseUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1"`
2. Set `memorySearch.provider: "openai"`
3. Run `resolveMemorySearchConfig`

### Expected

Embedding provider resolves to the Ollama adapter (model: "nomic-embed-text") instead of OpenAI adapter

### Actual (without fix)

Embedding provider always resolves to the OpenAI adapter regardless of `api` field

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: openai direct match, openai with api:ollama generic routing, openai with baseUrl fallback, auto/local/none providers, custom provider aliases
- Edge cases checked: Generic resolution returns same ID as provider name, generic resolution returns undefined, no registered adapter for resolved ID
- What you did NOT verify: Live Ollama endpoint roundtrip, multi-provider setups with both openai and ollama registered

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The fix operates at the config-resolution layer where routing decisions belong, and leverages the existing `resolveConfiguredGenericEmbeddingProviderId` path instead of adding new routing logic.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The change is bounded (8 lines in one function).
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `baseUrl` hostname detection in `getConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProvider` to auto-detect local endpoints. Rejected because it would couple core code to specific endpoint patterns; the `api` field is the canonical routing mechanism.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Analysis of root cause in `src/agents/memory-search.ts:194`, `src/plugins/embedding-provider-config.ts`, `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings-remote-client.ts`. Design reviewed with user before implementation.

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: A provider named "openai" with both `baseUrl` and `api` fields set could trigger ambiguous routing.
**Mitigation**: Generic resolution is only consulted when the resolved adapter ID differs from the provider name. If no better adapter is found, the direct match is used as fallback. Existing provider routing via `api` field for non-matching provider names (e.g., "ollama-5080" with `api: "ollama"`) is unchanged.
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
